records.ety: [LL. Lollardi, Lullardi, from Walter Lolhardus, a German; cf. LG. & D. lollen to mumble, to hum, sing in a murmuring strain; hence, OD. lollaerd a mumbler, i. e., of prayers or psalms, which was prob. the origin of the name. See Loll, Lull.]

defs: One of a sect of early reformers in Germany.
One of the followers of Wyclif in England.
quotes: By Lollards all know the Wyclifities are meant, so called from Walter Lollardus, one of their teachers in Germany.
